授業方法: General course format: This is NOT a lecture class. Lessons are highly student-centered involving individual, pair, and group work. Students are expected to actively contribute to the class, as well as complete their assignments in a timely manner before each class. This course will be taught in English only, and students are expected to use English as much as possible.

トピック.授業計画1: Please note that the weekly above schedule is for information purposes and may change throughout the semester. Any change will be announced to students in due time.
トピック.授業外学習2: Review each lesson and complete the regular homework assignments in order to be prepared for the following class. This should take approximately two hours per class lesson.

教科書/Required texts1: Paul Edmunds, Nancie McKinnon, Jeff Zeter
Building Skills for the TOEFL iBT 3rd Edition - Reading
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each class subject is arranged on the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school. By studying this course with DP/CP in mind, you will develop the abilities expected by each faculty and graduate school. The DP, CP, and curriculum table (list of classes, etc.) for each faculty/graduate school are posted in the link below, so please use them as a reference for your study plan. https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1 *Link to kwic (only current students can view)
